cards.back: A feature that allows you to provide a different implementation of a template class for a specific type or set of types.

cards.text:
To fully specialize a template class Storage<T> for type char*:
{{c1::template<>}}
{{c2::class Storage<char*> { ... };}}

cards.front: Does a class template specialization need to have the same member functions as the primary template?
cards.back: No. A specialization can have completely different member functions and data members than the primary template.

cards.question: What does this code do?
cards.answer: Provides a full specialization of Storage for bool type with a potentially different implementation.
cards.explanation: The specialization can optimize storage or behavior for bool, and doesn't need to match the primary template's interface.

cards.text: A class template specialization uses {{c1::template<>}} (empty angle brackets) because {{c2::all template parameters are specialized}}.

cards.front: If both a primary template and a matching specialization exist, which does the compiler use?
cards.back: The compiler uses the more specialized version (the specialization) when the types match.

cards.concept: When to use class template specialization
cards.explanation:
Use class template specialization when:
  • You need special handling for specific types (e.g., char* for string handling)
  • You can optimize for certain types (e.g., std::vector<bool> uses bit packing)
  • Certain types require fundamentally different implementations
examples: template<> class Storage<char*> { // Deep copy strings };

cards.front: Can you specialize just one member function of a template class without specializing the whole class?
cards.back: No. To change one member function for a specific type, you must fully specialize the entire class.

cards.text: The primary template must be {{c1::declared}} before you can create a specialization of it.

cards.front: What is a real-world example of class template specialization in the standard library?
cards.back: std::vector<bool> is a specialization that uses bit-packing to save space (though this is controversial due to non-standard behavior).

cards.question: What's wrong with this code?
cards.answer: Missing template<> declaration. Should be: template<> class Storage<char*> { ... };
cards.explanation: Class specializations need the template<> prefix to indicate they are template specializations, not regular classes.

cards.text: A class template specialization can have {{c1::completely different}} member functions and data members than the {{c2::primary template}}.
std::vector<bool> is a {{c1::specialization}} that uses {{c2::bit-packing}} to save space.
